title: SIP vs FD vs PPF — Which is Best?
meta_description: Compare SIP, Fixed Deposit, and PPF side by side. Returns, risk, lock-in, tax benefits — find which investment is best for your goals.

hero.one_liner: The three most popular investment options in India compared — find what works best for your goals and risk appetite

sections.heading: Quick Answer
content: There is no single best option — it depends on your goal and time horizon. For long-term wealth creation (5+ years), SIP wins. For safe, tax-free guaranteed returns over 15 years, PPF wins. For short-term savings (1-3 years) or emergency funds, FD wins.

The smartest approach is to use all three: FD for emergency fund (6 months expenses), PPF for guaranteed tax-free savings (max ₹1.5 lakh/year for 80C), and SIP for long-term goals like retirement, house down payment, or children's education.

sections.heading: Detailed Comparison
sections.footnote: SIP returns assume equity mutual funds. Debt fund SIPs give 6-8% with lower risk.

rows.label: You're a beginner
rows.value: Start with PPF (₹500/yr) + SIP in Nifty 50 Index Fund (₹500/mo). Add FD for emergency fund.

rows.label: You want tax savings
rows.value: PPF first (fully tax-free), then ELSS SIP for remaining 80C limit. 5yr FD as last option.

rows.label: You have short-term goal
rows.value: FD or liquid fund. Don't use SIP or PPF for goals under 3 years.

rows.label: You want maximum returns
rows.value: SIP in equity mutual funds. Accept 10-30% short-term drops for 12-15% long-term returns.

rows.label: You want zero risk
rows.value: PPF + FD only. You'll get 6.5-7.1% but your money is 100% safe.

sections.heading: Real Numbers: ₹5,000/month for 15 years
content: Here's what happens if you invest ₹5,000/month in each option for 15 years:
SIP at 12% return: You invest ₹9 lakh → Grows to approximately ₹25.2 lakh (2.8× your money)
FD at 7% return: You invest ₹9 lakh → Grows to approximately ₹15.8 lakh pre-tax, ~₹13.5 lakh after tax (1.5× your money)
PPF at 7.1% return: You invest ₹9 lakh → Grows to approximately ₹16.2 lakh completely tax-free (1.8× your money)
The difference is massive over 15 years. But remember — SIP returns are not guaranteed while PPF and FD returns are.

faqs.question: Can I invest in all three simultaneously?
faqs.answer: Yes, and most financial advisors recommend it. A common split: PPF for tax-free guaranteed base (₹1.5L/year), SIP for wealth creation (20% of income), FD for emergency fund (6 months expenses).

faqs.question: I only have ₹2,000/month. Where should I put it?
faqs.answer: ₹1,000 in SIP (Nifty 50 Index Fund) + ₹500 in PPF + keep ₹500 in savings until you build a small emergency FD. Adjust ratios as income grows.

faqs.question: Are SIP returns guaranteed?
faqs.answer: No. SIP invests in mutual funds which are market-linked. Returns can be negative in the short term. However, over 7+ years, equity SIPs have historically never given negative returns in India.

faqs.question: Is PPF return going to stay at 7.1%?
faqs.answer: The rate is reviewed quarterly by the government. It has ranged from 7.1% to 8.7% in the last decade. It can go up or down, but changes are usually small (0.1-0.2% at a time).

disclaimer: This article is for educational purposes only. Past returns do not guarantee future results. Consult a SEBI-registered financial advisor before investing.
